agent_memory.purpose: Nuanced concepts for a portfolio construction/stock picking agent (not a trading agent).

sections.context: Packaged-food P&Ls are dominated by agricultural inputs (cocoa, sugar, coffee, wheat, oils, proteins) and packaging. Most majors use commodity derivatives/forwards to smooth COGS, but programs vary (some don't use hedge accounting; gains/losses sit in corporate and create timing noise). Always map coverage horizon vs. guidance.
sections.investment_insight: Underwrite margin durability by aligning hedge coverage horizon with pricing cadence and guidance windows. Identify whether hedge gains/losses flow through COGS or corporate, and handicap quarter-to-quarter EPS volatility from timing mismatches.
metrics_raw: Map hedge coverage horizon vs guidance windows
Identify accounting location of hedge gains/losses (COGS vs corporate)

sections.topic: Acute Input Shocks Create Multi-Quarter Margin Air-Pockets
sections.context: Cocoa spiked to record highs in 2024–25 on West African crop disease and weather—material for chocolate/cookies/cereal makers. Price/mix often lags COGS for several quarters at some brands.
sections.investment_insight: Validate exposure mix and hedge roll-off schedules. Names with heavier cocoa/coffee/sugar mix and thin hedges face near-term margin compression; faster price-pack architecture and promo discipline can narrow the lag.
metrics_raw: Lag between COGS inflation and price/mix recovery: multi-quarter

sections.context: The U.S. sugar program (TRQs/marketing allotments) structurally lifts domestic sugar prices above world levels—important for confectionery, bakery, and categories using HFCS/sugar.
sections.investment_insight: Normalize input baskets for U.S. sugar policy; favor portfolios less sugar-intensive or with superior pass-through mechanisms.

sections.topic: Packaging Is a Material COGS Lever
sections.context: Resin (PET/HDPE/PP) often represents the majority of plastic packaging cost; aluminum/glass swings matter too. When resin cycles tighten, margin elasticity shows up quickly in spreads.
sections.investment_insight: Screen for contracts indexed to resin/aluminum and suppliers with scale. Packaging-light formats and redesigns can be hidden margin tailwinds.
metrics_raw: Resin share of plastic packaging costs: often majority
Aluminum/glass price swings flow through quickly

sections.context: Walmart/Costco/Kroger concentration can be >10–20% of a branded CPG's sales. OTIF/SQEP chargebacks (e.g., high delivery targets; penalties for failures) can leak gross-to-net and distort quarterly margins.
sections.investment_insight: Scrutinize customer concentration, OTIF/SQEP deduction trends, and supply-chain reliability. Small execution misses can produce outsized gross-to-net leakage.

sections.topic: Trade Spend Is Huge and Opaque
sections.context: Promotions, slotting, displays, and buy-downs typically run low-teens to 20%+ of gross sales. Mis-execution means a large share fails to earn its keep. Net price realization can swing on trade-spend cycles.
sections.investment_insight: Track scan-data lift vs. shipments and commentary on elevated trade investment. Prioritize ROI discipline on trade over sheer spend levels.
metrics_raw: Trade spend: low-teens to 20%+ of gross sales

sections.context: Pack resizing, multipacks, and channel-specific formats are the levers behind pricing power. Brands actively managing PPA with retailers show better volume retention after price hikes.
sections.investment_insight: Reward issuers with robust PPA test-and-learn cadence; penalize those relying on across-the-board list price with weak pack strategy.

sections.topic: Private Label Is the Real Share Enemy
sections.context: In down-cycle wallets, retailer brands take mix; 2024–25 saw private-label share gains and pressure on mid-tier brands. Some private-label manufacturers faced their own volume whipsaws as consumers traded down then bought less.
sections.investment_insight: Handicap private-label penetration by category and key account strategy. Moaty brands with taste/health credentials and superior execution withstand PL encroachment better.

sections.topic: Regulatory Step-Ups Create Non-Linearity in Costs/Sourcing
sections.context: Multiple rules raise compliance burdens and may reshape sourcing.
sections.investment_insight: Identify portfolios exposed to cocoa/coffee/palm/soy and additive reformulation risk; favor firms ahead on traceability and QA.
additional_notes: EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) imposes traceability obligations for cocoa/coffee/palm/soy supply chains starting for many firms in late 2025.
California AB 418 pushes certain additive reformulations.
FSMA Rule 204 (traceability) raises recall/QA costs; recalls still hover around hundreds per year across FDA/USDA.

sections.context: Evidence so far shows modest basket changes (e.g., fewer high-calorie snacks) in GLP-1 cohorts, with more sensitivity in indulgent categories.
sections.investment_insight: Do not overgeneralize; pressure is not uniform. Re-rate risk is higher for indulgent calorie-dense categories without credible better-for-you (BFY) pivots.

sections.context: Retailers are pruning long tails. Brands that earn shelf with velocity and differentiated pack sizes keep space and promo; others are culled.
sections.investment_insight: Favor management teams proactively simplifying SKU tails and reinvesting in winners; penalize cluttered portfolios with low-velocity SKUs.

sections.context: Dollar/club growth shifts price-mix and promo cadence; foodservice rebounds can cushion certain categories. Mix and integration (e.g., grain JVs) can buffer commodity cycles.
sections.investment_insight: Underwrite incremental margins by channel. Structural tilt toward club/dollar can compress brand ASPs but lift velocity; foodservice exposure can diversify volatility.

sections.context: For packaged meats (beef/pork/turkey), cattle/hog cycles and disease (avian flu) swing spreads. Margin recovery depends on herd rebuild timing and contract structures.
sections.investment_insight: Track USDA cycle commentary, exposure mix, and customer contract terms (cost-plus vs fixed).
metrics_raw: Herd rebuild timing drives spread normalization

sections.context: Some U.S. food names still use LIFO for portions of inventory; in high inflation, LIFO can depress GAAP gross margin but shift cash taxes timing.
sections.investment_insight: Normalize for LIFO/FIFO methodology and reserves when benchmarking margins and cash conversion.

sections.context: Forward-buying/trade loading around price changes and promo windows can create 1–2 quarter noise in reported revenue.
sections.investment_insight: Prioritize syndicated scanner data over shipments for run-rate demand. Read management's 'inventory in channel' color before extrapolating quarterly trends.
